title: NASA to add missions to SpaceX commercial crew contract
summary: NASA intends to increase missions under SpaceX's commercial crew contract as a precaution, given potential delays in Boeing's spacecraft certification for ISS missions.

title: Blue Origin completes investigation into New Glenn launch failure
summary: Blue Origin has completed the investigation into the failure of its New Glenn rocket's third flight, allowing launches to resume. The company has cleared the vehicle for future missions.

title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the first Starship V3 on May 22, achieving most of the test objectives for its suborbital flight. This marks a significant milestone in the development of the next-generation Starship vehicle.

title: Space Force awards Viasat, SES $437 million for military satellite network
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded Viasat and SES a $437 million contract to develop four small geostationary satellites for the Protected Tactical Satcom-Global program. This initiative aims to enhance secure military communications.

title: NASA unveils sweeping reorganization
summary: NASA announced a major reorganization on May 22, merging mission directorates and reshuffling personnel. This marks one of the agency's most significant restructuring efforts in recent years.

title: Leveraging AUKUS and southern geography: building Australia’s dual-use space infrastructure for strategic resilience
summary: Australia is developing dual-use space infrastructure, including the Deep Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC) Site 1 in Western Australia, to enhance strategic resilience in collaboration with AUKUS partners. Full operational capability for DARC is expected by 2027.

title: SpaceX IPO filing casts Starlink Mobile as future wireless challenger
summary: SpaceX's IPO filing highlights Starlink Mobile as a potential competitor to terrestrial wireless networks, positioning it as a future wireless challenger. The service aims to provide direct-to-smartphone connectivity comparable to urban mobile networks.

title: Rocket Lab launches ninth Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its ninth radar imaging satellite for Synspective. The company also announced plans to sell up to $3 billion in stock to fund future initiatives.

title: Rocket Lab wins first GEO satellite production contract from U.S. Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $90 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to build and operate two GEO satellites with optical payloads. This marks Rocket Lab's first GEO satellite production contract.

title: Ground equipment problem scrubs Starship launch attempt
summary: SpaceX canceled the first launch attempt of its newest Starship vehicle on May 21 due to a ground equipment issue. The scrubbed launch marks a delay in the vehicle's maiden flight.

title: Lunar Strike offers a pessimistic, and worryingly realistic look at the future of space travel
summary: The article 'Lunar Strike' presents a pessimistic and realistic view of the future of space travel. It highlights concerns about the challenges and risks associated with lunar missions.

title: The most common type of planet in the galaxy may not look anything like Earth on the inside
summary: A new study suggests that the most common type of planet in the galaxy may not have a dense metallic core like Earth. Instead, their internal structures could be vastly different from what we assume.

title: China reveals 3-person Shenzhou 23 crew, including Hong Kong's 1st astronaut
summary: China announced the three astronauts for the Shenzhou 23 mission, including Hong Kong's first astronaut. The crew is set to launch on May 24.

title: 'Rick and Morty' Season 9: Release date & how to watch Adult Swim’s zany sci-fi satire
summary: Adult Swim announced the release date for 'Rick and Morty' Season 9, teasing a return of the zany sci-fi satire. The show's unique blend of humor and science fiction continues to captivate audiences.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 211 — Oysters in Space
summary: The latest episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with Jacob Scoccimarro of Monolith Space about space-related topics, including 'Oysters in Space'.

title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12: Don't miss these stunning photos from the launch of the most powerful Starship yet
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its 12th Starship flight, featuring the more powerful Starship V3 variant from Starbase. The launch provided stunning visuals as the rocket aims to support future lunar astronaut missions.

title: 'For All Mankind's' Costa Ronin on ruling Mars, honoring cosmonaut history, and learning to tie his tie (interview)
summary: The interview with Costa Ronin from 'For All Mankind' discusses the challenges of ruling Mars, the importance of honoring cosmonaut history, and practical skills like tying a tie. The conversation highlights the blend of historical reverence and futuristic governance in the series.

title: Massive 'X' and 'V' shapes will appear on the moon tonight — here's how to see them
summary: Massive 'X' and 'V' shapes will be visible on the moon during the first quarter phase. This phenomenon can be observed with a telescope or binoculars.

title: Watch China launch Shenzhou 23 astronauts to its Tiangong space station on May 24
summary: China is set to launch the Shenzhou 23 mission to its Tiangong space station on May 24, relieving an overdue crew and potentially marking the start of the country's first year-long stay in orbit.

title: SpaceX just launched Starship V3 — its most powerful megarocket yet — into space for the 1st time in spectacular Flight 12 test (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3, the most powerful rocket ever built, for its first test flight. The event showcased the megarocket's advanced technology and capabilities.

title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
summary: NASA is seeking creative partners to help tell the stories of its missions, including Artemis Moon missions and nuclear propulsion. The agency issued a call for proposals on May 21 for filmmakers, songwriters, and other storytellers.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
summary: NASA will broadcast a live spacewalk by two Roscosmos cosmonauts outside the International Space Station on May 27, starting at 10:15 a.m. EDT and lasting five hours. Coverage begins at 9:45 a.m. on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA’s YouTube channel.

title: Webb Studies Star Clusters
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope released a near-infrared image of a spiral arm of Messier 51 (M51) as part of a study of nearly 9,000 star clusters. The study reveals that more massive star clusters tend to emerge more frequently.

title: Keeping NASA Flying: Ground Crews Ensure Aircraft Readiness
summary: NASA relies on ground crews to maintain aircraft readiness for research flights and science campaigns. The agency recently added two new aircraft at its Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California.

title: NASA to Compete Contract for Jet Propulsion Laboratory Management
summary: NASA plans to compete the next contract for managing Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), currently operated by Caltech since its inception. The move aims to ensure accountability and value for U.S. taxpayers.

title: NASA Announces Realignment to Accelerate Mission Delivery
summary: NASA announced an agencywide realignment to accelerate mission delivery and align with the National Space Policy. The changes aim to improve efficiency and speed in achieving national space objectives.

title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
summary: A new material discovered at NASA's Glenn Research Center could enable astronauts to melt Moon rocks and extract valuable resources like metals and oxygen for future lunar missions. This innovation supports NASA's goal of utilizing lunar resources to reduce payload weight and sustain long-term exploration.

title: Hubble Captures Galaxy Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy cluster with galaxies of various shapes and sizes. The image also features foreground stars with distinctive diffraction spikes.

title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft’s Orbit
summary: NASA will showcase a mission to boost the orbit of the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory using Northrop Grumman’s Pegasus XL rocket and a Katalyst robotic spacecraft called LINK. The event will be held at NASA’s Wallops Flight Facility in Virginia on June 17.

title: Shenzhou 23 set to launch new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: China is preparing to launch the Shenzhou 23 mission to send a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 5, 2022.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, demonstrating impressive performance during its maiden flight. The mission marked a significant milestone for SpaceX's next-generation rocket system.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled worldwide in the next week, including SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, Arianespace's Vega C, and SpaceX's Starship Flight 12.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: The European Space Agency's SMILE mission was launched aboard Arianespace's Vega C rocket to study Earth's magnetosphere. This mission aims to enhance understanding of the solar wind's interaction with Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a significant advancement for future space missions.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively planning Artemis III, a critical crewed mission to the Moon. The mission is in deep processing stages, including key Human Landing System (HLS) tests.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company's growth marked a significant milestone in its expansion.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the ISS on its third attempt, following a previous docking by Progress MS-34. The mission marks another resupply effort to the International Space Station.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past 50 years. This commemorative publication celebrates ESA's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and validate materials for spacecraft to ensure they withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to support ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to improve training, operations, and simulations for space exploration, while also engaging the public. This technology is opening new pathways for how space missions are conducted and communicated.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for a spacecraft's parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out.' This test ensures the parachute's functionality under extreme conditions.
company: Not explicitly mentioned in the provided text

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life beneath the Martian surface while adhering to strict planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology is being developed for space applications, potentially revolutionizing satellite servicing and debris removal. This innovation could enhance the safety and efficiency of in-space operations.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that current military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities in the country.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: The ESA Chief urges Europe to double its funding for space initiatives, emphasizing the need for increased investment despite recent growth in public funds.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's recent stakeholder survey revealed that improved lunar technology is the top priority for space tech investments. The survey aimed to identify key technical challenges in the space community.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has approved two new Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research with cost-effective solutions. This initiative demonstrates Europe's commitment to affordable yet impactful space science.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO, expected next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system could accelerate the nuclear arms race despite its intended purpose of protecting America.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve Chinese researchers, posing potential security risks. The findings highlight concerns over collaborations with China in space-related scientific projects.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in design reviews to enhance efficiency in engineering production. The company aims to address inefficiencies that cause program delays due to complex decision-making.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year depending on the scenario.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, has launched a new satellite bus business line. The initiative targets low-cost, high-volume, and high-power satellite missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO in late March, emphasizing the company's focus on cloud-native and interoperable ground technology amid a dynamic market.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has inaugurated a new space domain awareness (SDA) software named Borealis, which is now operational six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris for the U.K. government.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract to build the first Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for a U.S. Space Force space domain awareness (SDA) mission. This marks Rocket Lab's entry into GEO satellite manufacturing.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, showcasing messaging capabilities on Android smartphones via its L-band satellite technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth funding round to support its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company announced this initiative on May 21.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to identify and develop innovative solutions in orbital logistics.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an agentic AI geospatial query system that allows users to ask natural language questions about geospatial data, such as identifying new construction sites in Florida. This technology aims to enhance accessibility and usability of geospatial information.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. The collaboration aims to enhance operational efficiency in airport operations.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial IPO documents, revealing its financials for the first time. This marks a significant step toward the company's public offering.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: China's hosting of the 2027 World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) is raising concerns among U.S. space operators due to its potential influence over critical satellite industry agenda items. The event is expected to heavily focus on satellite-related developments.
